mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, labor market slack signals weak consumer spending and recession risks, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where unemployment exceeds 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDP and retail sales fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate recovering manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, generating positive market returns specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains coincide with negative 20-day returns in over 60 percent of post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates below 0.25 percent indicate extreme monetary accommodation that fuels liquidity-driven asset price inflation, producing rapid positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain flat or negative in more than half of the observations when the policy rate stays below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates signals robust job creation without wage inflation pressure, supporting steady equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined employment expansion and unemployment decline yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 900 thousand units reflect strong residential investment demand, which amplifies consumer confidence and drives positive market returns during housing expansion cycles.
failure_condition: Housing starts above 900 thousand units fail to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of housing expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side momentum that translates into short-term positive equity returns through improved corporate output expectations.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production changes are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases across all market conditions.
